news.title: Faith joins football as Spain and Argentina face off for World Cup title
news.lede: (OSV News) — As the July 19 World Cup final approaches, players from two historically Catholic nations can count not only on the prayers of their fans, but also on a rich web of Catholic threads surrounding the showdown between Spain and Argentina. From the tournament’s origins to the faith openly lived by players and coaches on both sides, this final carries a distinctly Catholic dimension. The match at New Jersey’s MetLife stadium brings together two football giants whose paths to the final have been marked not only by sporting excellence, but also by visible expressions of Catholic faith. If anyone wondered where the precision combined with clearly having fun on the pitch comes from in the Spanish team, striker Ferran Torres gave a sense of the answer when he spoke publicly about his Catholic faith.

news.title: Boy, 13, dies during World Cup celebrations in Spain
news.lede: A 13-year-old boy was killed after part of a fountain collapsed in Salamanca province, northwestern Spain, during the country's World Cup victory celebrations. Two other young people were injured, emergency services told the BBC, including one child who was taken to hospital with a possible broken bone in his leg. Youth were celebrating Spain's victory inside the Árbol Gordo fountain in Salamanca's Ciudad Rodrigo, when it partially collapsed around 00:30 local time (23:30 BST). "What was supposed to be a celebration for the victory in the Spanish National Football Team's World Cup has turned into a tragedy," Ciudad Rodrigo's town hall posted on social media.

news.title: Taking stock of 2026 World Cup: Football's biggest show yet
news.lede: A worthy world champion, a call from the US president, security concerns and a few pleasant surprises. The 2026 World Cup is over. So what will we remember about global football's biggest World Cup ever? Football didn't have it easy at the 2026 World Cup in the United States of America, Mexico, and Canada.

news.title: Top 30: from half a million World Cup photos, why I love these the most
news.lede: Our picture editor chooses his favourite images from the tournament including Lionel Messi’s cathedral, that delicious diving header from Jude Bellingham and a humbling scene in Gaza. What makes a great World Cup photograph? Now there’s a question with myriad answers. Composition, newsworthiness, emotional resonance, expert timing and luck can all contribute to varying degrees.

news.title: The 1994 World Cup influenced a generation of Americans. What will 2026’s impact be?
news.lede: There's no denying a landmark World Cup will leave an indelible mark on America. But what will be its legacy? And who will it galvanize?

news.title: 2026 World Cup: Spain 1-0 Argentina (AET): Ferran Torres' extra-time winner secures Spain's second title
news.lede: Spain won their second World Cup title after Ferran Torres' 106th-minute smashed finish earned a 1-0 win against defending champions Argentina in the 2026 final. Spain were deserved winners. Torres scored with Spain's 20th shot while the game ended without Argentina registering a single shot on target. Lionel Messi touched the ball just 54 times in a match that took three hours to complete and featured a 27-minute half-time break.

news.title: Our 2026 World Cup Team of the Tournament
news.lede: The biggest World Cup ever reached its conclusion on Sunday night as Spain beat Argentina to be crowned world champions. Spain celebrate a second world title, ending Argentina’s bid for back-to-back triumphs. The 2026 tournament will be remembered as a competition where records fell, and the game’s biggest stars shone. Following the final, we’ve put together our 2026 World Cup Team of the Tournament.

news.title: Curtis Murayama: World Cup has been great, but now it’s time for U.S. football
news.lede: The World Cup has come and gone, offering fans gripping thrillers, wildly entertaining theatre and riveting finishes.
